Explore these SystemVerilog and Python files in the RISC-V-Vector project (root: /home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/ic-lab-duth-RISC-V-Vector) and summarize each. Be thorough ("very thorough").

Files to read:
- rtl/vector/vmu.sv (vector memory unit top)
- rtl/vector/vmu_ld_eng.sv (load engine)
- rtl/vector/vmu_st_eng.sv (store engine)
- rtl/vector/vmu_tp_eng.sv (tile prefetch engine)
- rtl/vector/vld_st_buffer.sv
- vector_simulator/vector_sim_top.sv (TB top)
- vector_simulator/vector_driver.sv (TB driver)
- vector_simulator/sim_generator.py (emulation script)
- vector_simulator/compile_vector_simulator.do (build script)
- vector_simulator/examples/vvadd/instrs.csv (example program)

For each file report:
1. Module/script name and one-line purpose
2. Key parameters
3. Main interfaces / IO
4. Core mechanism (load/store engines, acquire-release, arbitration, decoupled execution, how the driver feeds instructions, how sim_generator decodes CSV into memory files, what the .do script compiles)
5. Notable structures
6. Approximate line count

Return a concise structured summary per file. Do NOT dump whole files. Quote only key signal names and a couple representative lines from the CSV.

320	        .wrtbck_data_o    (tp_wb_data            ),
321	        .wrtbck_ticket_o  (tp_wb_ticket          ),
322	        //RF Writeback Probing Interface
323	        .wrtbck_reg_a_o   (wrtbck_prb_reg_o[2]   ),
324	        .wrtbck_locked_a_i(wrtbck_prb_locked_i[2]),
325	        .wrtbck_ticket_a_i(wrtbck_prb_ticket_i[2]),
326	        .wrtbck_reg_b_o   (wrtbck_prb_reg_o[3]   ),
327	        .wrtbck_locked_b_i(wrtbck_prb_locked_i[3]),
328	        .wrtbck_ticket_b_i(wrtbck_prb_ticket_i[3]),
329	        //Unlock Interface
330	        .unlock_en_o      (toepl_unlock_en       ),
331	        .unlock_reg_a_o   (toepl_unlock_reg_a    ),
332	        .unlock_reg_b_o   (toepl_unlock_reg_b    ),
333	        .unlock_ticket_o  (toepl_unlock_ticket   ),
334	        //Request Interface
335	        .grant_i          (tp_grant              ),
336	        .req_en_o         (tp_request            ),
337	        .req_addr_o       (toepl_req_addr        ),
338	        .req_microop_o    (toepl_req_microop     ),
339	        .req_size_o       (toepl_req_size        ),
340	        .req_ticket_o     (toepl_req_ticket      ),
341	        // Incoming Data from Cache
342	        .resp_valid_i     (mem_resp_valid_i      ),
343	        .resp_ticket_i    (mem_resp_i.ticket     ),
344	        .resp_size_i      (mem_resp_i.size       ),
345	        .resp_data_i      (mem_resp_i.data       ),
346	        //Sync Interface
347	        .is_busy_o        (is_busy[2]            )
348	    );
349	
350	    //=======================================================
351	    //Keep track of the oldest LD/TP instruction in the engine
352	    //and provide the grants for the writeback access
353	    //=======================================================
354	    logic [1:0] is_older               ;
355	    logic [1:0] nxt_is_older           ;
356	    logic [1:0] high_priority_grant    ;
357	    logic       any_high_priority_grant;
358	    logic       new_ld_starts          ;
359	
360	    //Requests by the oldest instruction are considered high priority
361	    assign wb_grant = ({2{ any_high_priority_grant}} & high_priority_grant) |
362	                      ({2{~any_high_priority_grant}} & wb_request          );
363	
364	    assign any_high_priority_grant = |high_priority_grant;
365	    assign high_priority_grant     = is_older & wb_request;
366	
367	    assign new_ld_starts = push_load | push_toepl;
368	    always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
369	        if(~rst_n) begin
370	            is_older <= '0;
371	        end else if(new_ld_starts) begin
372	            is_older <= nxt_is_older;
373	        end
374	    end
375	
376	    always_comb begin
377	        if(new_ld_starts) begin //new load started
378	            nxt_is_older = is_busy[2] ? 2'b10 : //the old toepl is the oldest
379	                                        2'b01;  //the new load is the oldest
380	        end else begin //new toepl started (or nothing started)
381	            nxt_is_older = is_busy[0] ? 2'b01 : //the old load is the oldest
382	                                        2'b10;  //the new toepl is the oldest
383	        end
384	    end
385	
386	    //=======================================================
387	    //Keep track of the oldest instruction in the engine
388	    //and provide the grants for the memory access
389	    //=======================================================
390	    logic [1:0] push_data;
391	    logic [1:0] pop_data;
392	    logic push;
393	    logic pop;
394	
395	    assign load_starts  =  push_load & ~push_store & ~push_toepl;
396	    assign store_starts = ~push_load &  push_store & ~push_toepl;
397	    assign toepl_starts = ~push_load & ~push_store &  push_toepl;
398	
399	    assign load_ends    = ~is_busy[0];
400	    assign store_ends   = ~is_busy[1];
401	    assign toepl_ends   = ~is_busy[2];
402	
403	    assign any_request = ld_request | st_request | tp_request;
404	    assign any_grant   = ld_grant   | st_grant   | tp_grant;
405	
406	    assign push_data = load_starts  ? 2'b01 :
407	                       store_starts ? 2'b10 : 2'b11;
408	    assign push = load_starts | store_starts | toepl_starts;
409	    // keep active instructions in vMU
410	    fifo_duth #(
411	        .DW   (2),
412	        .DEPTH(3)
413	    ) fifo_duth (
414	        .clk      (clk       ),
415	        .rst      (~rst_n    ),
416	        .push_data(push_data ),
417	        .push     (push      ),
418	        .ready    (fifo_ready),
419	        .pop_data (pop_data  ),
420	        .valid    (valid     ),
421	        .pop      (pop       )
422	    );
423	
424	    assign ld_grant =  valid & cache_ready_i & pop_data == 2'b01 & ld_request;
425	    assign st_grant =  valid & cache_ready_i & pop_data == 2'b10 & st_request;
426	    // prefetcher gets access to idle cycles as well
427	    assign tp_grant = (valid & cache_ready_i & pop_data == 2'b11 & tp_request) |
428	                      (~ld_grant & ~st_grant & cache_ready_i     & tp_request);
429	
430	    assign pop      = (valid & pop_data == 2'b01 & load_ends)  |
431	                      (valid & pop_data == 2'b10 & store_ends) |
432	                      (valid & pop_data == 2'b11 & toepl_ends);
433	
434

436	    `include "vmu_sva.sv"
437	`endif
438	
439	endmodule

1	/*
2	* @info Load/Store Buffer Module
3	* @info Top Modules: data_cache.sv
4	*
5	* @author VLSI Lab, EE dept., Democritus University of Thrace
6	*
7	* @brief  Used in the non-blocking Data Cache.
8	*
9	* @param DATA_WIDTH    : # of Data Bits (default 32 bits)
10	* @param ADDR_BITS     : # of Address Bits (default 32 bits)
11	* @param BLOCK_ID_START: # of Bit from which the Block ID starts
12	* @param R_WIDTH       : # of Register Bits (default 6 bits)
13	* @param MICROOP_WIDTH       : # of Microoperation Bits (default 5 bits)
14	* @param ROB_TICKET    : # of ROB Ticket Bits (default 3 bits)
15	* @param DEPTH         : # of Entries in Buffer (default 8 entries)
16	*/
17	module vld_st_buffer #(
18	    parameter DATA_WIDTH     = 32,
19	    parameter ADDR_BITS      = 32,
20	    parameter BLOCK_ID_START = 5 ,
21	    parameter R_WIDTH        = 5 ,
22	    parameter MICROOP_WIDTH  = 7 ,
23	    parameter TICKET_WIDTH   = 4 ,
24	    parameter SIZE_WIDTH     = 3 ,
25	    parameter DEPTH          = 4
26	) (
27	    input  logic                     clk             ,
28	    input  logic                     rst_n           ,
29	    //Write Port
30	    input  logic                     push            ,
31	    input  logic [    ADDR_BITS-1:0] write_address   ,
32	    input  logic [   DATA_WIDTH-1:0] write_data      ,
33	    input  logic [ TICKET_WIDTH-1:0] write_ticket    ,
34	    input  logic [MICROOP_WIDTH-1:0] write_microop   ,
35	    input  logic [   SIZE_WIDTH-1:0] write_size      ,
36	    //Update Port for fetch status
37	    input  logic                     valid_update_i  ,
38	    input  logic [    ADDR_BITS-1:0] update_address_i,
39	    //Head Output
40	    input  logic                     pop             ,
41	    output logic                     head_is_store   ,
42	    output logic                     head_is_fetched ,
43	    output logic [    ADDR_BITS-1:0] head_address    ,
44	    output logic [   DATA_WIDTH-1:0] head_data       ,
45	    output logic [MICROOP_WIDTH-1:0] head_microop    ,
46	    output logic [ TICKET_WIDTH-1:0] head_ticket     ,
47	    output logic [   SIZE_WIDTH-1:0] head_size       ,
48	    //Status Port
49	    output logic                     valid_o         ,
50	    output logic                     ready_o
51	);
52	
53	    localparam INFO_WIDTH = TICKET_WIDTH + MICROOP_WIDTH + SIZE_WIDTH;
54	    // #Memory Arrays#
55	    logic [     DEPTH-1:0][ ADDR_BITS-1:0] saved_address     ;
56	    logic [     DEPTH-1:0][DATA_WIDTH-1:0] saved_data        ;
57	    logic [     DEPTH-1:0][INFO_WIDTH-1:0] saved_info        ;
58	    logic [INFO_WIDTH-1:0]                 head_info, search_info;
59	    logic [     DEPTH-1:0]                 saved_valid       ;
60	    logic [     DEPTH-1:0]                 saved_isstore     ;
61	    logic [     DEPTH-1:0]                 saved_is_fetched  ;
62	    logic [     DEPTH-1:0]                 matched_update    ;
63	    logic [     DEPTH-1:0]                 head_oh           ;
64	    logic                                  write_isstore     ;
65	    logic                                  will_update       ;
66	
67	
68	    // #Internal Signals#
69	    logic [DEPTH-1:0] head, tail;
70	    logic [DEPTH:0]   stat_counter;
71	
72	    assign valid_o = ~stat_counter[0];
73	    assign ready_o = ~stat_counter[DEPTH];
74	
75	    assign write_isstore = write_microop[4:3] === 2'b11;
76	    assign head_oh       = 1 << head;
77	
78	    //Tail Management (moves on new pops)
79	    always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin : TailManagement
80	        if(!rst_n) begin
81	            tail <= 1;
82	        end else begin
83	            if (push) tail <= {tail[DEPTH-2:0], tail[DEPTH-1]};
84	        end
85	    end
86	    // Head Management (moves when it points to an invalid entry)
87	    always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin : HeadManagement
88	        if(!rst_n) begin
89	            head <= 1;
90	        end else begin
91	            if (pop) begin
92	                head <= {head[DEPTH-2:0], head[DEPTH-1]};
93	            end
94	        end
95	    end
96	    //Status Counter Management
97	    always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin : statCounter
98	        if(!rst_n) begin
99	            stat_counter <= 1;
100	        end else begin
101	            if (push & !pop) begin
102	                // shift left status counter (increment)
103	                stat_counter <= stat_counter << 1;
104	            end else if (!push &  pop) begin
105	                // shift right status counter (decrement)
106	                stat_counter <= stat_counter >> 1;
107	            end
108	        end
109	    end
110	    // Memory Management (Data Write)
111	    always_ff @(posedge clk) begin : MemoryData
112	        for (int i = 0; i < DEPTH; i++) begin
113	            if(push && tail[i]) begin
114	                saved_address[i] <= write_address;
115	                saved_data[i]    <= write_data;
116	                saved_isstore[i] <= write_isstore;
117	                saved_info[i]    <= {write_size,write_ticket,write_microop};
118	            end
119	        end
120	    end
121	    // Memory Management (Validity)
122	    always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin : MemoryValidity
123	        if(!rst_n) begin
124	            saved_valid <= 'b0;
125	        end else begin
126	            for (int i = 0; i < DEPTH; i++) begin
127	                if(push && tail[i]) begin
128	                    saved_valid[i] <= 1'b1;
129	                end else if(pop && head[i]) begin
130	                    saved_valid[i] <= 1'b0;
131	                end
132	            end
133	        end
134	    end
135	    //Search the Locations to update as Fetched
136	    always_comb begin : matchUpdate
137	        for (int i = 0; i < DEPTH; i++) begin
138	            matched_update[i] = saved_valid[i] ? (update_address_i[ADDR_BITS-1:BLOCK_ID_START]==saved_address[i][ADDR_BITS-1:BLOCK_ID_START]) : 1'b0;
139	        end
140	    end
141	    assign will_update = |matched_update & valid_update_i;
142	    // Memory Management (is_fetched)
143	    always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin : MemoryFetched
144	        if(!rst_n) begin
145	            saved_is_fetched <= 'b0;
146	        end else begin
147	            for (int i = 0; i < DEPTH; i++) begin
148	                if(push && tail[i]) begin
149	                    saved_is_fetched[i] <= 1'b0;
150	                end else if(will_update && matched_update[i]) begin
151	                    saved_is_fetched[i] <= 1'b1;
152	                end
153	            end
154	        end
155	    end
156	
157	    //HEAD OUTPUTS
158	    // Pick the Address Output
159	    and_or_mux #(
160	        .INPUTS(DEPTH    ),
161	        .DW    (ADDR_BITS)
162	    ) mux_addr (
163	        .data_in (saved_address),
164	        .sel     (head         ),
165	        .data_out(head_address )
166	    );
167	    // Pick the Data Output
168	    and_or_mux #(
169	        .INPUTS(DEPTH     ),
170	        .DW    (DATA_WIDTH)
171	    ) mux_data (
172	        .data_in (saved_data),
173	        .sel     (head      ),
174	        .data_out(head_data )
175	    );
176	
177	    //Pick the MICROOP_WIDTH/Destination Output
178	    and_or_mux #(
179	        .INPUTS(DEPTH     ),
180	        .DW    (INFO_WIDTH)
181	    ) mux_info (
182	        .data_in (saved_info),
183	        .sel     (head      ),
184	        .data_out(head_info )
185	    );
186	
187	    assign head_microop = head_info[0 +: MICROOP_WIDTH];
188	    assign head_ticket  = head_info[MICROOP_WIDTH +: TICKET_WIDTH];
189	    assign head_size    = head_info[MICROOP_WIDTH + TICKET_WIDTH +: SIZE_WIDTH];
190	
191	    //Pick the is_fetched Output
192	    and_or_mux #(
193	        .INPUTS(DEPTH),
194	        .DW    (1    )
195	    ) mux_is_fetched (
196	        .data_in (saved_is_fetched),
197	        .sel     (head            ),
198	        .data_out(head_is_fetched )
199	    );
200	
201	    //Pick the is_store Output
202	    and_or_mux #(
203	        .INPUTS(DEPTH),
204	        .DW    (1    )
205	    ) mux_is_store (
206	        .data_in (saved_isstore),
207	        .sel     (head         ),
208	        .data_out(head_is_store)
209	    );
210	
211	
212	    assert property (@(posedge clk) disable iff(!rst_n) push |-> ready_o) else $error("ERROR:vLD_ST Buffer: Push on Full");
213	    assert property (@(posedge clk) disable iff(!rst_n) pop |-> valid_o) else $error("ERROR:vLD_ST Buffer: Pop on Empty");
214	
215	    assert property (@(posedge clk) disable iff(!rst_n) valid_update_i |-> ~$isunknown(update_address_i)) else $error("ERROR:vLD_ST Buffer: update_address_i");
216	    assert property (@(posedge clk) disable iff(!rst_n) ~$isunknown(will_update)) else $error("x-check:vLD_ST Buffer: will_update");
217	    assert property (@(posedge clk) disable iff(!rst_n) ~$isunknown(push)) else $error("x-check:vLD_ST Buffer: push");
218	    assert property (@(posedge clk) disable iff(!rst_n) ~$isunknown(pop)) else $error("x-check:vLD_ST Buffer: pop");
219	    assert property (@(posedge clk) disable iff(!rst_n) ~$isunknown(|saved_valid)) else $error("x-check:vLD_ST Buffer: saved_valid");
220	
221	endmodule

content.text:
---

## FILE SUMMARIES

### 1. rtl/vector/vmu.sv (~440 lines)

**Module:** `vmu` (Vector Memory Unit top)

**Purpose:** Top-level integration of vector memory operations, dispatching to load/store/tile-prefetch engines with arbitration.

**Key Parameters:**
- `REQ_DATA_WIDTH=256`, `VECTOR_REGISTERS=32`, `VECTOR_LANES=8`
- `DATA_WIDTH=32`, `ADDR_WIDTH=32`, `VECTOR_TICKET_BITS=4`

**Main Interfaces:**
- **Instruction in:** `valid_in`, `instr_in` (memory_remapped_v_instr), `ready_o`
- **Cache interface:** `mem_req_valid_o`, `mem_req_o` (out); `cache_ready_i`, `mem_resp_valid_i`, `mem_resp_i` (in)
- **RF read ports:** `rd_addr_0/1/2_o`, `rd_data_0/1/2_i`, `rd_pending_*_i`, `rd_ticket_*_i`
- **RF writeback:** `wrtbck_en_o`, `wrtbck_reg_o`, `wrtbck_data_o`, `wrtbck_ticket_o`
- **Unlock/probe interfaces:** `unlock_en_o`, `unlock_reg_a/b_o`, `unlock_ticket_o`; `wrtbck_prb_reg_o`, `wrtbck_prb_locked_i`, `wrtbck_prb_ticket_i`

**Core Mechanism:**
- **Engine dispatch:** Classifies instructions as `is_load` (`LD_BIT` set), `is_store`, or `is_toepl` (microop `7'b1110011` or `7'b1010011`). Pushes to appropriate engine.
- **Reconfiguration:** When `instr_in.reconfigure`, simultaneously pushes to all three engines.
- **Arbitration:** Uses `fifo_duth` (3-deep FIFO) to track in-flight engines. Grants memory access based on FIFO ordering: `ld_grant`, `st_grant`, `tp_grant`. Prefetcher gets idle cycles.
- **Writeback arbitration:** Two-bit `is_older` register tracks oldest load/tile-prefetch for writeback priority. `wb_grant` prioritizes older instruction.
- **Unlock multiplexing:** Routes unlock signals from the active engine based on `wb_grant`.

**Notable Structures:**
- Instantiates `vmu_ld_eng`, `vmu_st_eng`, `vmu_tp_eng` with identical parameters
- `fifo_duth` for instruction tracking
- Status tracking: `is_busy[2:0]`, `tail`, `fifo_ready`

---

### 2. rtl/vector/vmu_ld_eng.sv (~580 lines)

**Module:** `vmu_ld_eng` (Vector Load Engine)

**Purpose:** Executes vector load instructions with address generation, element tracking, and decoupled writeback via dual scratchpad rows.

**Key Parameters:**
- Same as VMU top; derived `MAX_SERVED_COUNT = min(MAX_MEM_SERVED_LIMIT, VECTOR_REGISTERS)`

**Main Interfaces:**
- **Instruction in:** `valid_in`, `instr_in`, `ready_o`
- **RF read (indexed):** `rd_addr_o`, `rd_data_i`, `rd_pending_i`, `rd_ticket_i`
- **RF writeback:** `wrtbck_req_o`, `wrtbck_grant_i`, `wrtbck_en_o`, `wrtbck_reg_o`, `wrtbck_data_o`, `wrtbck_ticket_o`
- **Writeback probe:** `wrtbck_reg_a/b_o`, `wrtbck_locked_a/b_i`, `wrtbck_ticket_a/b_i`
- **Memory request:** `grant_i`, `req_en_o`, `req_addr_o`, `req_microop_o`, `req_size_o`, `req_ticket_o`
- **Memory response:** `resp_valid_i`, `resp_ticket_i`, `resp_size_i`, `resp_data_i`

**Core Mechanism:**
- **Address generation:** Supports three modes:
  - `OP_UNIT_STRIDED`: Increment by element size (multi-element requests)
  - `OP_STRIDED`: Increment by stride
  - `OP_INDEXED`: Add offset from vector register (`rd_data_i[element_index +: 32]`)
- **Dual-row scratchpad:** `logic [1:0][VECTOR_LANES-1:0][DATA_WIDTH-1:0] scratchpad` - enables decoupled execution. While row 0 writes back, row 1 can fetch.
- **Element tracking:** Three bitvectors per row: `pending_elem` (awaiting fetch), `served_elem` (fetched), `active_elem` (awaiting writeback).
- **Request/response ticket:** `req_ticket_o = {current_row, current_pointer_wb_r}` - used to match responses.
- **Expansion loops:** Handles VL > VECTOR_LANES via `current_exp_loop_r`, incrementing destination registers (`rdst_r`, `src2_r`).
- **Writeback readiness:** `row_0_ready` = all active elements served AND register locked with matching ticket.
- **Unlock timing:** Unlocks source registers when writeback completes.

**Notable Structures:**
- Scoreboard with per-element pending/active/served tracking
- Stall counter: `total_ld_stalled_due_is` for interlock stalls
- Scratchpad response unpacking with size-based handling (8/16/32-bit)

---

### 3. rtl/vector/vmu_st_eng.sv (~400 lines)

**Module:** `vmu_st_eng` (Vector Store Engine)

**Purpose:** Executes vector store instructions, gathering data from registers and issuing memory writes.

**Key Parameters:** Same as load engine

**Main Interfaces:**
- Similar to load but with **two RF read ports**: `rd_addr_1_o` (data), `rd_addr_2_o` (indexed offsets)
- **No writeback interface** (stores don't write back to RF)
- **Memory request includes data:** `req_data_o`

**Core Mechanism:**
- **Data gathering:** Reads source register (`rd_data_1_i`) and optionally offset register (`rd_data_2_i`) for indexed stores.
- **Multi-element stores:** For unit-strided, packs multiple elements into single request. Size-based packing: 8/16/32-bit selection from each 32-bit lane.
- **Pending element tracking:** Single `pending_elem` vector (no dual-row since no writeback).
- **Unlock timing:** Unlocks registers on `start_new_loop` (per-vregister boundary) or `current_finished`.
- **Can-be-interleaved:** Only for non-indexed operations (`can_be_inteleaved_o`)

**Notable Structures:**
- `el_served_count` calculation based on memory operation type
- Expansion loop increments `src1_r` and `src2_r` each iteration

---

### 4. rtl/vector/vmu_tp_eng.sv (~560 lines)

**Module:** `vmu_tp_eng` (Tile Prefetch Engine - Toeplitz)

**Purpose:** Prefetches 2D tile data for convolution-like operations, with speculative prefetch and validation.

**Key Parameters:** Same as other engines

**Main Interfaces:** Similar to load engine (has writeback)

**Core Mechanism:**
- **FSM states:** `IDLE`, `ACTIVE`, `PREFETCH`
  - `IDLE` → `ACTIVE`: Start new instruction
  - `ACTIVE` → `PREFETCH`: Begin speculative prefetch after instruction completes
  - `PREFETCH` → `ACTIVE`: Continue if prefetch matches (`continue_instruction`), else abort
- **2D address calculation:** 
  - `row_base = row_num * image_size_r`
  - `addr_offset = win_row_base + win_col_num_r`
  - `current_addr = base_addr_r + addr_offset`
- **Speculative prefetch:** Predicts next tile access based on kernel progression. Validates via `instr_mismatch_found = (nxt_base_addr !== base_addr_r)`.
- **Toeplitz config:** Microop `7'b1110011` configures `image_size_r`, `kernel_size_r`.
- **Kernel window tracking:** `win_row_num_r`, `win_col_num_r` track position within kernel. Frame advance when `win_row_num_r === kernel_size_r`.

**Notable Structures:**
- Dual-row scratchpad like load engine
- Prefetch validation logic
- Image/kernel size registers

---

### 5. rtl/vector/vld_st_buffer.sv (~220 lines)

**Module:** `vld_st_buffer` (Load/Store Buffer)

**Purpose:** Non-blocking buffer for memory operations, tracking pending status and supporting out-of-order completion updates.

**Key Parameters:**
- `DATA_WIDTH=32`, `ADDR_BITS=32`, `DEPTH=4`
- `TICKET_WIDTH=4`, `MICROOP_WIDTH=7`, `SIZE_WIDTH=3`

**Main Interfaces:**
- **Push:** `push`, `write_address`, `write_data`, `write_ticket`, `write_microop`, `write_size`
- **Pop:** `pop`, `head_address`, `head_data`, `head_microop`, `head_ticket`, `head_size`, `head_is_store`, `head_is_fetched`
- **Update:** `valid_update_i`, `update_address_i` (marks entries as fetched)
- **Status:** `valid_o`, `ready_o`

**Core Mechanism:**
- **Circular buffer:** Head/tail pointers, status counter for empty/full detection.
- **Fetched status update:** Searches buffer for matching block address (`update_address_i[ADDR_BITS-1:BLOCK_ID_START]`) and sets `saved_is_fetched`.
- **Store detection:** `write_isstore = write_microop[4:3] === 2'b11`
- **Output mux:** `and_or_mux` selects head entry for output.

**Notable Structures:**
- Arrays: `saved_address`, `saved_data`, `saved_info`, `saved_valid`, `saved_isstore`, `saved_is_fetched`
- Assertions for push/pop on full/empty

### 7. vector_simulator/vector_driver.sv (~80 lines)

**Module:** `vector_driver` (Instruction Feeder)

**Purpose:** Reads pre-decoded instruction files from `sim_generator.py` and feeds them to the vector processor.

**Key Parameters:**
- `DEPTH=SIM_VECTOR_INSTRS` (autogenerated)

**Main Interfaces:**
- **Output:** `valid_o`, `instr_o` (to_vector struct), `pop_i` (from processor)

**Core Mechanism:**
- **Memory arrays:** 12 arrays loaded via `$readmemb` from `decoder_results/instrs/*.txt` files:
  - `data1_list`, `data2_list`, `immediate_list`
  - `destination_list`, `operand_a/b/c_list`
  - `microop_list`, `fu_list`, `reconfigure_list`
  - `maxvl_list`, `vl_list`
- **Dummy instruction detection:** Special encoding (reconfigure + all-zero fu/microop/vl/maxvl) used to inject bubble cycles for scalar core emulation.
- **Head pointer management:** Increments on `pop_i` or dummy instruction.

**Notable Structures:**
- Simple head-pointer based sequencer
- No backpressure (assumes processor ready when valid)

### 8. vector_simulator/sim_generator.py (~560 lines)

**Script:** `sim_generator.py` (CSV Decoder)

**Purpose:** Converts RISC-V vector assembly-like CSV into binary memory files for simulation.

**Key Parameters (defaults/CLI):**
- `FILE_NAME='instrs.csv'`, `AVL=16`, `VECTOR_LANES=8`
- `TOTAL_BUBBLE_CYCLES=4`

**Core Mechanism:**
- **CSV parsing:** `get_instruction_list()` reads CSV, removes comments (lines starting with `#` or `//`)
- **Microop encoding:** `get_microop()` maps mnemonic to 7-bit binary (e.g., `vld`→`1000000`, `vadd`→`0000001`)
- **Operand parsing:** `get_operand()` handles registers (`v0`→`0`) and immediates (`#16`→`16`)
- **VL expansion:** Calculates `REPEATS = ceil(AVL / maxvl)` where `maxvl = max_vregs * VECTOR_LANES`
- **File generation:** Writes 15 files to `decoder_results/<filename>/`:
  - Field files: `microop_output.txt`, `fu_output.txt`, `destination_output.txt`, etc.
  - `decoded_output.txt`: Human-readable decoding
  - `autogenerated_params.sv`: `localparam int SIM_VECTOR_INSTRS = <total>`
- **Bubble injection:** After each instruction repeat, inserts `TOTAL_BUBBLE_CYCLES` dummy entries with special encoding (microop=`1111111`, fu=`11`, reconfigure=`1`)

**Notable Structures:**
- Switch dictionaries for microop and FU encoding
- Binary conversion: `get_binary_32()`, `get_binary_5()`
- Initial `reconfigure` instruction written first to set `maxvl`
